title: daphne uva-ursi var. monticola
intro: Daphne uva-ursi var. monticola, also known as Mountain Cranberry, is a low-growing evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to astringent and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of arbutin. The health benefits of Daphne uva-ursi var. monticola include its potential to soothe urinary tract issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as urinary tract infections and skin issues.
